prose:
Method–provenance summary (fukui-reactivity)

• Property: Condensed Fukui functions f⁺/f⁻/f⁰ and dual descriptor (unitless) for each atom; headline value reported below is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺.
• Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical self-consistent tight-binding; no Gaussian basis, no exchange–correlation functional).
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (ferrocene.xyz, 21 atoms). Single-point charge analyses only; no geometry optimization was performed (GFN2-xTB//input geometry).
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent model requested).
• Charge / multiplicity: 0 / 1 (closed-shell singlet), with the N±1 states treated as doublets (charge ±1, multiplicity 2).
• Software: xtb 6.6 via chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill.
• Convergence & caveats: SCF converged. Integrity status = ok; trustworthy = true. Density fitting not applicable to xtb.

Live run log (written during execution): /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpfzq0osfq/live.out
Result JSON: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpfzq0osfq/live.json

Headline result

Most electrophilic site: Fe (atom 11) with f⁺ = 0.22885456 (unitless).

Warnings (verbatim from chemkit)
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present

Interpretation

The GFN2-xTB Mulliken condensed Fukui analysis indicates that the iron center is by far the most electrophilically and nucleophilically susceptible site in ferrocene (f⁺ ≈ 0.23, f⁻ ≈ 0.26). Cyclopentadienyl carbons are significantly less reactive (f⁺, f⁻ ≈ 0.012–0.014). These indices provide qualitative guidance on likely reactive centers; quantitative values are method-dependent.
